skill.name: superpower-zustand
skill.description: Use a typed StoreBuilder pattern to create Zustand stores with separated state, factory-based actions, immer updates, and optional persistence.
skill.summary: Build consistent, typed Zustand stores with a reusable StoreBuilder pattern.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and backticked TypeScript identifiers, not shell execution. The skill includes optional browser persistence and a relative fetch example, which are legitimate Zustand patterns but should not be used for secrets without explicit user review.

low_findings.title: Optional Browser Persistence Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill documents localStorage and sessionStorage persistence for Zustand state. This is a normal feature, but generated stores could expose sensitive client data if users persist secrets or tokens.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly discuss browser storage persistence. The risk is limited because no credentials are shown and persistence is configurable.

low_findings.title: Relative Fetch Example in Documentation
low_findings.description: The pattern guide includes an async action that fetches from a relative application endpoint. No external host or credential exfiltration is present, but generated examples may perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The fetch call is direct and visible, but it targets a relative path. This supports a low-risk finding rather than a high-risk network concern.

low_findings.title: Overly Directive Skill Language
low_findings.description: The skill uses mandatory and critical wording to enforce its architectural pattern. It does not ask the evaluator to skip analysis, but the directive tone could overconstrain agent behavior when selected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is clearly directive, but it is scoped to Zustand implementation guidance. I did not find evidence that it tries to override security review or system instructions.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent Zustand stores that stay type-safe as features grow. This skill provides a StoreBuilder pattern for actions, immer updates, non-reactive access, and optional persistence.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of Zustand stores with a reusable StoreBuilder utility.
Separates state fields from action methods using TypeScript types.
Applies immer-style state updates through Zustand middleware.
Shows optional persistence with storage keys, versions, and partial state selection.
Documents non-reactive get, set, and subscribe access outside React components.
Provides examples for simple stores, preference stores, todo stores, and factory return types.
limitations: Focused only on Zustand store architecture for React and TypeScript projects.
Does not provide migration tooling for existing stores.
Persistence examples require careful review before storing sensitive data.
Async examples use generic application endpoints and need project-specific API handling.

use_cases.title: Create Feature Stores
use_cases.description: Build a new feature store with clear state types, actions, and immer updates.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Patterns
use_cases.description: Apply one repeatable Zustand pattern across a codebase to reduce style drift.

use_cases.title: Add Persistent Preferences
use_cases.description: Persist selected client preferences while keeping transient state out of storage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Store
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a small React feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Zustand store for this feature using the StoreBuilder pattern. Include state types, actions, exports, and immer-style updates.

prompt_templates.title: Add Persistence Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Add persistence to this Zustand store using StoreBuilder. Persist only safe preference fields and explain what should stay transient.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor an Existing Store
prompt_templates.scenario: Move from inline actions to the factory pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this existing Zustand store to the StoreBuilder pattern. Keep behavior the same and separate state from actions.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Async Store
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced store with loading, error, and API state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a typed Zustand store for this async workflow using StoreBuilder. Include loading, error handling, actions, and non-reactive access where useful.

output_examples.input: Create a store for a counter with increment and decrement actions.
output_examples.output: A TypeScript Zustand store using StoreBuilder, with count state, separated actions, an exported hook, and immer-style updates.

output_examples.input: Persist theme and language preferences but not loading state.
output_examples.output: A preference store plan that persists only theme and language fields, with transient UI state excluded from browser storage.

output_examples.input: Refactor my todo store to use the required pattern.
output_examples.output: A refactoring outline that separates todo state from todo actions and keeps async loading and error behavior intact.

best_practices: Persist only non-sensitive fields that must survive browser refreshes.
Define state and actions separately before calling the StoreBuilder factory.
Use immer draft mutations consistently inside StoreBuilder set callbacks.
anti_patterns: Do not store access tokens, session secrets, or private data in browser persistence.
Do not mix action methods into the initial state object.
Do not return replacement state objects from immer-style set callbacks.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build typed Zustand stores with a reusable StoreBuilder pattern and separated actions.

faq.question: Does it require TypeScript?
faq.answer: The examples and utility are written for TypeScript projects.

faq.question: Can it persist state?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows optional persistence with storage keys, versions, and partial field selection.

faq.question: Is browser persistence safe for secrets?
faq.answer: No. Avoid storing tokens, credentials, or sensitive personal data in localStorage or sessionStorage.

faq.question: Does it support code outside React components?
faq.answer: Yes. The StoreBuilder pattern exposes get, set, and subscribe for non-reactive access.

faq.question: Does it replace Zustand itself?
faq.answer: No. It is a pattern and helper utility built on top of Zustand and its middleware.
